tutorial.markdown.formatting:
# Basic Formatting

Welcome to **notty**! This note demonstrates the *basic formatting* features of the Markdown parser.

## Bold and Italics

Use `**double asterisks**` for **bold**, `*single asterisks*` for *italic*, and `***both***` for ***bold and italic*** text.

## Headers

Headers are created with `#` symbols:

# H1 — Main Title
## H2 — Section
### H3 — Subsection

## Task Lists

Checkboxes keep track of your progress:

- [x] Create a notty account
- [x] Read this tutorial note
- [ ] Write your first note
- [ ] Explore [[Advanced Elements]]

#tutorial #formatting
tutorial.markdown.advanced:
# Advanced Elements

This note showcases external links, tables, and images.

## External Links

Visit the [Markdown Guide](https://www.markdownguide.org) for a comprehensive reference.

## Table

A simple feature comparison:

| Feature | Plain Text | Markdown |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Bold / Italics | No | Yes |
| Headings | No | Yes |
| Task Lists | No | Yes |
| Wikilinks | No | Yes |

## Image

![A wide mountain landscape with snow-capped peaks reflected in a calm alpine lake](https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1506905925346-21bda4d32df4?w=800)

Continue to [[Interconnectivity]] to learn about linking notes together.

#tutorial #advanced
tutorial.markdown.wikilinks:
# Interconnectivity

One of notty's most powerful features is **Wikilinks** — a simple syntax for connecting notes into a personal knowledge network.

## How Wikilinks Work

Wrap any note title in double square brackets:

```
[[Note Title]]
```

notty resolves the link automatically if a note with that exact title exists.

## Links to the Other Tutorial Notes

- [[Basic Formatting]] — covers bold, italics, headers, and task lists.
- [[Advanced Elements]] — covers external links, tables, and images.

## Ghost Links

If you write `[[A Note That Does Not Exist Yet]]`, notty marks it as a *ghost link*. Clicking it opens a new-note form pre-filled with that title — a great way to grow your knowledge base organically.

#tutorial #wikilinks
